original_datasets.name: Christensen 2018 semantic fluency and openness data
original_datasets.notes: Two files are used: FINAL open.csv (individual openness measures, including LATENT) and FINAL fluency.csv (animal-naming responses with columns vf_an_01..vf_an_35). Numerous additional columns exist beyond those listed. Data are already preprocessed per the reanalyst (labeled FINAL).

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without any restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Construct semantic networks by openness groups, apply TMFG-like filtering, and compare ASPL via bootstrapped subnetworks; additionally, bootstrap participant subgroups to relate ASPL to mean openness, and bin openness into windows to relate bin-level ASPL to openness.
preprocessing_steps: Split participants into high vs low openness using the median of LATENT.
From fluency responses, construct a participant-by-word binary matrix (lowercase, drop missing/placeholder entries).
Retain words that appear in at least 2 participants in each group when constructing group networks.
Compute pairwise cosine similarity between word co-occurrence vectors.
Apply a TMFG/PMFG-like planar filtering to obtain sparse unweighted adjacency matrices (binarize edges).
For Approach 1: Bootstrap subnetworks by randomly selecting 90% of nodes (1000 iterations) for both groups and compute ASPL on each subnetwork.

For Approach 2: Bootstrap subgroups of participants (n=20), build a network for each subgroup, compute ASPL and record subgroup mean LATENT; test ASPL ~ openness association and control for word count.
For Approach 3: Bin participants by LATENT in width 0.10 windows, build a network for each bin, compute ASPL, and test ASPL ~ bin mean LATENT.

key_choices.outcome_measure: Average Shortest Path Length (ASPL) computed on the largest connected component of the constructed semantic network.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Openness to experience (LATENT): median-split group indicator for Approach 1; continuous LATENT for Approaches 2 and 3.
key_choices.data_processing: Text normalization to lowercase and removal of placeholder entries, construction of binary co-occurrence matrix, cosine similarity computation, planar backbone filtering, subnetwork bootstrapping.
key_choices.model_choice: Paired t-test for group ASPL differences (Approach 1); Pearson correlation and OLS regression for ASPL ~ openness (Approaches 2 and 3).
key_choices.inference_rule: p-value from paired t-test, Pearson correlation, and regression coefficients.

outcome.name: ASPL
outcome.definition: Average Shortest Path Length of the semantic network's largest connected component.

main_predictor.name: LATENT (and group indicator)
main_predictor.definition: Openness to experience score LATENT; group indicator defined by LATENT > median(LATENT) for group comparisons.

analysis_code.notes: Python rewrite of the provided R Task1 script implementing the three approaches. Uses a planar filtering approximation to TMFG and basic text normalization for fluency responses.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: In the partial network that you use, the rate of nodes remaining should be 90%.

analysis_path.path_description: Reproduce Approach 1 only: construct group semantic networks, apply TMFG-like filtering, bootstrap subnetworks retaining 90% of nodes (1000 iterations), and compare ASPL via paired t-test.
preprocessing_steps: Median-split participants on LATENT into high vs low openness groups.
Construct participant-by-word binary matrix (lowercase, remove placeholders).
Select words that occur in at least 2 participants in each group.
Compute word-wise cosine similarity matrices for each group.
Apply planar filtering to obtain unweighted adjacency for each group.
Bootstrap subnetworks by selecting 90% of nodes (1000 iterations) in both groups.
Compute ASPL for each subnetwork and conduct a paired t-test of ASPL between groups.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: High vs low openness group by median split on LATENT.
key_choices.model_choice: Paired t-test on bootstrapped ASPL values.

outcome.name: ASPL
outcome.definition: Average Shortest Path Length of the semantic network's largest connected component.

main_predictor.name: Openness group
main_predictor.definition: Binary group indicator for LATENT > median(LATENT).

analysis_code.notes: Python rewrite focused on the node-retention-90% bootstrap comparison required for Task2.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the 90% node-retention bootstrap approach only; do not modify the planned method.

files.data/Multi100_reproduction_ZXD19_task1__: Python rewrite of Task1. Builds semantic networks by openness groups, applies planar filtering, computes ASPL, runs Approach 1 (paired t-test with 90% node bootstrap), Approach 2 (participant bootstrap with correlation/OLS), and Approach 3 (binning by LATENT). IO set to /app/data.
files.data/Multi100_reproduction_ZXD19_task2__: Python rewrite of Task2. Implements only Approach 1 with 90% node bootstrap and paired t-test. IO set to /app/data.

codebase.notes: Python rewrites approximate the R pipeline: text cleaning is simplified (lowercase and removal of placeholders) relative to SemNetCleaner, and TMFG is approximated by a greedy planar backbone filter using NetworkX planarity checks. Ensure /app/data contains FINAL fluency.csv and FINAL open.csv.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset in /app/data. Produce structured outputs including ASPL means per group, paired t-statistics and p-values for Approach 1, and correlation/regression outputs for Approaches 2 and 3 in Task1.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Task1: mean ASPL (high vs low), paired t-statistic, p-value; Pearson r and p for ASPL ~ openness in participant bootstraps and binned analyses; OLS coefficients and p-values. Task2: mean ASPL (high vs low), paired t-statistic, p-value.
